prompt:
Construct a Meridian Check-In & Commerce Operations Suite supporting offline-first school district check-in programs with an integrated merch store and reporting workflow. The Yew-based web UI provides distinct homepages by role: Student and Parent users can sign in with a local username and password, view today’s check-in window (for example, 7:00 AM–9:30 AM), complete a one-tap check-in with immediate on-screen confirmation, and see reminders for upcoming deadlines or missed check-ins; notification preferences include subscription toggles and a Do Not Disturb window (default 9:00 PM–6:00 AM) that suppresses non-critical in-app prompts. Teachers and Academic Staff review submissions in a queue, apply an approval decision with a required reason on denials, and can filter by school, homeroom, or date range; users see approval results in an in-app inbox with frequency controls (immediate, daily digest at 5:00 PM, or weekly on Fridays). Administrators work in an operations console to manage products, orders, users, and marketing settings, monitor a real-time order dashboard, and access KPI views such as daily sales, average order value, and repeat purchase rate; exports are available as local files for a selected date range (e.g., 03/01/2026–03/31/2026). Across the UI, access is strictly role-based with clear menu visibility and inline “not authorized” feedback, and sensitive screens support a quick-lock that re-prompts for password after 10 minutes of inactivity on shared devices.

On the system side, the backend uses Actix-web to implement a decoupled REST-style API consumed by the Yew frontend over the local network or localhost, with all data persisted in PostgreSQL for fully offline operation. Authentication is local-only: passwords must be at least 12 characters, stored using salted Argon2 hashes; login is rate-limited to 5 attempts per username per 15 minutes with a 30-minute lockout, and CAPTCHA/SMS/email/third-party sign-in are not used. RBAC enforces permissions at menu, API, and data-scope levels (e.g., teachers scoped to assigned classes; admins scoped by district or campus), with support for account disable/freeze, account deletion requests requiring admin approval, and blacklist rules that immediately block access. A configuration center stores editable dictionaries and parameters such as shipping fee ($6.95 flat, editable), points earning rate (1 point per $1.00, editable), and campaign feature toggles; changes are versioned and logged with who/when/what for auditability. Scheduled jobs run inside the service to auto-close unpaid orders after 30 minutes, generate low-stock alerts when inventory drops below 10 units, and produce scheduled reports (daily/weekly) into an export folder; query performance is protected with indexed date and scope fields and a default maximum report range of 12 months per request. Operational logging includes access, error, and business audit logs retained for 180 days, and the console exposes controlled entry points to run database backup and restore from local storage; backups are AES-256 encrypted with a locally managed key, and exports mask personally identifiable data by default (showing only last 4 digits of IDs) unless an admin with explicit “PII Export” permission overrides it.
